articles.headline: Government Unveils Detailed Implementation Roadmap for National Green Hydrogen Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) released a comprehensive roadmap for the phased implementation of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The roadmap outlines specific targets for production capacity, electrolyzer manufacturing, and reduction in production costs over the next decade.
• It emphasizes public-private partnerships, R&D funding, and skill development to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
• Key focus areas include developing demand-side applications in hard-to-abate sectors like fertilizers, refining, and steel, and creating export opportunities.
• The mission aims to contribute significantly to India's energy security, decarbonization goals, and job creation.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental Pollution and Degradation
Conservation
Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched by MNRE.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Focus on reducing production costs and developing demand-side applications.
Key sectors for application: fertilizers, refining, steel.
Part of India's broader climate change and energy security strategy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and achieving net-zero targets.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in the implementation of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Evaluate the potential socio-economic impacts, including job creation and industrial growth, from a thriving green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine how the mission aligns with global climate action and India's international commitments.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with renewable energy sources like solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Sanjay Roy Convicted in 2024 Kolkata Rape and Murder Case by Sealdah Court
articles.explainer:
• Sanjay Roy has been convicted by the Sealdah court in Kolkata, West Bengal, for the 2024 Kolkata rape and murder case.
• The verdict comes after a fast-tracked trial, highlighting the judiciary's commitment to swift justice in heinous crimes.
• The case had drawn significant public attention and outrage, prompting calls for stricter laws and better law enforcement.
• Legal experts note the importance of forensic evidence and witness testimonies in securing the conviction.
• The judgment is expected to be followed by sentencing, which will determine the quantum of punishment.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization
Social empowerment
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in ensuring swift justice in cases of sexual assault and murder in India.
Analyze the role of public outcry and media in expediting judicial processes in high-profile cases.
Examine the effectiveness of existing laws and judicial mechanisms in protecting women and delivering justice.
Suggest reforms needed in the criminal justice system to prevent such crimes and ensure timely convictions.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a law book with a blurred background of a court building, symbolizing justice.
articles.trivia: India's first all-women police station was established in Kozhikode, Kerala, in 1973.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Society
prelimsSyllabus.area: Role of women and women’s organization

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at Current Level Amidst Global Economic Uncertainty
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest review, deciding to maintain the benchmark repo rate at its current level.
• The decision comes against the backdrop of persistent global economic uncertainties and a cautious approach to domestic inflation management.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to balance growth impulses with inflation control, citing geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to remaining accommodative while ensuring inflation remains within the target band.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the RBI, with future policy actions dependent on evolving economic data and global cues.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC maintains repo rate.
Decision influenced by global economic uncertainty and inflation management.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst current economic conditions.
Discuss the potential impacts of this monetary policy stance on various sectors of the Indian economy (e.g., banking, industry, consumers).
Evaluate the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in a volatile global environment.
Examine the role of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India's economic governance.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India logo on a building facade with financial graphs overlaid, symbolizing economic policy.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India and Japan Hold Strategic Dialogue, Emphasize Economic Security and Indo-Pacific Stability
articles.explainer:
• High-level delegations from India and Japan held their annual Strategic Dialogue in New Delhi, focusing on enhancing bilateral cooperation.
• Discussions centered on strengthening economic security, supply chain resilience, and collaborative efforts for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to deepening defense cooperation, including joint exercises and technology transfers.
• Key areas of collaboration identified include critical and emerging technologies, green energy, and infrastructure development.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of interests between India and Japan in regional and global affairs.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue held in New Delhi.
Focus areas include economic security, Indo-Pacific stability, defense cooperation.
Collaboration in critical technologies, green energy, infrastructure.
Both nations are key partners in the Quad grouping.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-Japan strategic partnership in ensuring stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how enhanced economic security cooperation between India and Japan can mitigate global supply chain vulnerabilities.
Evaluate the potential for India and Japan to collaborate on critical and emerging technologies (CETs) and its implications for global technological leadership.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in deepening defense ties between the two countries.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed prominently, symbolizing diplomatic relations.
articles.trivia: Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects, notably the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail corridor.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests

articles.headline: ISRO Intensifies Preparations for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focus on Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission.
• This mission is envisioned as India's first lunar sample return mission, aiming to bring back lunar soil and rock samples to Earth for detailed scientific analysis.
• Engineers and scientists are focusing on developing advanced robotics, precision landing technologies, and a robust sample containment system.
• Chandrayaan-4 builds upon the successes of Chandrayaan-1, 2, and 3, further solidifying India's capabilities in lunar exploration.
• The mission is crucial for understanding the Moon's geological history and potential resources, contributing significantly to global space science.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-4 is ISRO's planned lunar sample return mission.
Aims to collect and return lunar soil and rock samples.
Follows Chandrayaan-1, 2, and 3 missions.
Involves advanced robotics and precision landing technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar sample return missions for advancing planetary science and understanding the Moon's evolution.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in executing a lunar sample return mission and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its implications for global space leadership.
Examine the potential benefits of lunar resource exploration for future human spaceflight and industrial applications.
articles.imageDescription: Artist's impression of the Chandrayaan-4 lander and rover on the lunar surface, collecting samples.
articles.trivia: India became the fourth country to successfully land on the Moon with Chandrayaan-3 in August 2023.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Hear Petitions Seeking Greater Transparency in Electoral Bonds Scheme
articles.explainer:
• A special bench of the Supreme Court is scheduled to hear a batch of petitions challenging the Electoral Bonds Scheme.
• The petitions argue that the scheme lacks transparency, potentially leading to anonymous funding of political parties and impacting free and fair elections.
• Petitioners advocate for disclosure of donor identities to ensure accountability and prevent quid pro quo arrangements.
• The government has previously defended the scheme, citing donor privacy and curbing black money in political funding.
• The outcome of these hearings could have significant implications for political funding reforms and electoral transparency in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court hearing petitions on Electoral Bonds Scheme.
Petitions challenge the scheme on grounds of lack of transparency.
Government defends the scheme citing donor privacy.
Electoral bonds introduced in 2018.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds Scheme in the context of electoral transparency and democratic principles.
Discuss the role of the judiciary in upholding the principles of free and fair elections in India.
Analyze the impact of anonymous political funding on electoral integrity and the potential for corruption.
Suggest alternative models for political funding that balance transparency with donor privacy and curb the use of black money.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building with legal documents and a balance scale in the foreground, symbolizing justice and law.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from 1951 to 1952.

articles.headline: National Logistics Policy Shows Promising Progress, Targets Further Reduction in Costs
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry released a progress report on the National Logistics Policy (NLP), highlighting significant improvements.
• The report indicates a reduction in logistics costs as a percentage of GDP, attributed to improved infrastructure and technology integration.
• Key initiatives like the Unified Logistics Interface Platform (ULIP) and the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan have driven efficiency.
• The policy aims to further streamline supply chains, reduce transit times, and enhance India's competitiveness in global trade.
• Future focus includes greater private sector participation, skill development in logistics, and adoption of green logistics practices.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: National Logistics Policy (NLP) progress report released.
Aims to reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP.
Key initiatives: ULIP and PM Gati Shakti.
Focus on improving infrastructure and technology integration.
mainsPoints: Assess the impact of the National Logistics Policy on India's economic competitiveness and ease of doing business.
Discuss how PM Gati Shakti and ULIP contribute to multimodal connectivity and efficiency in the logistics sector.
Analyze the challenges that still persist in the Indian logistics sector and suggest further policy interventions.
Examine the role of technology and digitalization in transforming India's logistics landscape.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various modes of transport (road, rail, sea, air) connected by digital lines, symbolizing integrated logistics.
articles.trivia: India's logistics sector is one of the largest globally, contributing significantly to the country's GDP.

articles.headline: India-Central Asia Summit Focuses on Enhanced Connectivity and Regional Security
articles.explainer:
• Leaders from India and five Central Asian nations convened for a virtual summit, emphasizing closer cooperation in various sectors.
• A major theme was the enhancement of connectivity, including proposals for new transport corridors and digital linkages.
• Discussions also covered regional security challenges, counter-terrorism efforts, and the situation in Afghanistan.
• Cultural and people-to-people exchanges were highlighted as crucial for strengthening bilateral ties.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration affirming commitment to a comprehensive and sustained partnership.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Central Asia Summit held.
Focus on connectivity, regional security, counter-terrorism.
Central Asian nations: Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan.
Joint declaration issued for comprehensive partnership.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Central Asia for India's foreign policy and economic interests.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in developing robust connectivity pathways between India and Central Asian countries.
Examine the role of India-Central Asia cooperation in addressing regional security concerns, particularly regarding Afghanistan.
Evaluate India's 'Connect Central Asia' policy and its achievements so far.
articles.imageDescription: A world map highlighting India and the Central Asian region, with lines indicating trade routes and connectivity.
articles.trivia: The ancient Silk Road historically connected India with Central Asia, facilitating trade and cultural exchange.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests

articles.headline: National Curriculum Framework Implementation Begins with Focus on Foundational Learning
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced the phased implementation of the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for school education.
• The initial phase focuses on foundational stage education, emphasizing activity-based learning, multilingualism, and holistic development.
• NCF aligns with the recommendations of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, aiming for a flexible, multidisciplinary, and experiential learning approach.
• Teacher training and development of new pedagogical resources are critical components of this implementation.
• The framework seeks to reduce rote learning and foster critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving skills among students.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: National Curriculum Framework (NCF) implementation begins.
Focus on foundational stage education.
Aligned with National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Emphasizes activity-based learning and multilingualism.
Aims to reduce rote learning.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key features and objectives of the National Curriculum Framework (NCF) in line with NEP 2020.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a new curriculum framework across diverse educational ecosystems in India.
Evaluate the potential impact of NCF on student learning outcomes, critical thinking, and holistic development.
Examine the role of teacher training and technological integration in the successful adoption of the new curriculum.
articles.imageDescription: Children in a classroom engaged in interactive learning, with colorful educational materials.
articles.trivia: The first National Education Policy in India was formulated in 1968.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Expands Scope to Include Biodiversity Conservation and Wetland Restoration
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) announced an expanded mandate for the Namami Gange program.
• Beyond pollution abatement, the mission will now place a greater emphasis on biodiversity conservation along the Ganga basin.
• Initiatives include the restoration of wetlands, protection of aquatic species like the Gangetic Dolphin, and afforestation drives.
• The move aims to adopt a more holistic 'river basin management' approach, integrating ecological health with water quality.
• Collaboration with local communities and scientific institutions will be crucial for the success of these new objectives.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Namami Gange Mission expands to include biodiversity conservation and wetland restoration.
Focus on protecting aquatic species like Gangetic Dolphin.
Adopts a holistic 'river basin management' approach.
NMCG is the implementing agency for Namami Gange.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of the Namami Gange Mission from pollution abatement to a comprehensive river basin management approach.
Analyze the ecological significance of biodiversity conservation and wetland restoration in the Ganga basin.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing such large-scale environmental programs that involve multiple stakeholders and diverse ecosystems.
Examine the role of community participation and scientific research in the success of river rejuvenation efforts.
articles.imageDescription: A serene view of the Ganga river with lush green banks and a Gangetic Dolphin partially visible.
articles.trivia: The Gangetic Dolphin is India's National Aquatic Animal and is an indicator of the health of the river ecosystem.

articles.headline: INS Vikrant Set for First Major Operational Deployment in Indian Ocean Region
articles.explainer:
• India's first indigenously-built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, is preparing for its maiden major operational deployment.
• The deployment will significantly enhance India's naval power projection capabilities in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• It marks a critical milestone in India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (self-reliant India) initiative in defense manufacturing.
• The carrier will operate with a full complement of fighter jets, helicopters, and support vessels, forming a Carrier Battle Group.
• This move underscores India's strategic intent to safeguard its maritime interests and act as a net security provider in the IOR.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
prelimsPoints: INS Vikrant is India's first indigenously-built aircraft carrier.
Set for its first major operational deployment.
Enhances India's naval power projection in the Indian Ocean Region.
A key example of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defense.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of indigenous aircraft carriers like INS Vikrant for India's maritime security and regional influence.
Analyze how INS Vikrant's deployment contributes to India's role as a net security provider in the Indian Ocean Region.
Evaluate the progress and challenges of India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in the defense sector, with specific reference to naval capabilities.
Examine the geopolitical implications of increased Indian naval presence in the IOR amidst evolving regional dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: The majestic INS Vikrant sailing at sea with fighter jets on its deck, symbolizing naval power.
articles.trivia: INS Vikrant was built by Cochin Shipyard Limited, a public sector shipyard.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Policy to Promote Decentralized Solar Parks and Rooftop Solar
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power introduced a new policy aimed at accelerating the development of decentralized solar parks and rooftop solar installations.
• The policy provides financial incentives, simplified regulatory procedures, and land aggregation support for smaller solar projects.
• It seeks to empower local communities and reduce transmission losses by encouraging generation closer to consumption centers.
• This initiative is a crucial step towards achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and promoting energy access.
• The policy also emphasizes 'solarisation' of agricultural feeders and government buildings.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Conservation
Environmental Pollution and Degradation
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: New policy for decentralized solar parks and rooftop solar launched.
Provides financial incentives and simplified regulations.
Aims to reduce transmission losses and promote local generation.
Part of India's renewable energy targets and energy access goals.
mainsPoints: Discuss the advantages of decentralized solar power generation in achieving energy security and reducing carbon footprint.
Analyze the challenges in scaling up rooftop solar installations in India and how the new policy addresses them.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits of promoting solar energy at the local level, especially for rural communities.
Examine how India's solar energy policies contribute to its international climate commitments and leadership in renewable energy.
articles.imageDescription: Houses with solar panels on their rooftops, indicating widespread adoption of rooftop solar energy.
articles.trivia: India is home to the world's largest solar power plant, Bhadla Solar Park, located in Rajasthan.

articles.headline: India Forges Global Partnerships to Export Digital Public Infrastructure Model
articles.explainer:
• India is actively pursuing bilateral and multilateral partnerships to share its successful Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model with other developing nations.
• The 'India Stack' components like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are being showcased as examples of scalable and inclusive digital solutions.
• The initiative aims to foster digital transformation in partner countries, promoting financial inclusion and efficient public service delivery.
• This diplomatic push positions India as a leader in digital governance and a proponent of open-source, interoperable digital frameworks.
• Capacity building and technical assistance are key aspects of these partnerships, ensuring effective adoption and localization.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
prelimsPoints: India exporting its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model.
Key components: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker (India Stack).
Aims to promote digital transformation and financial inclusion in partner countries.
Positions India as a leader in digital governance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model as a global public good for developing nations.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of exporting the 'India Stack' to countries with diverse technological and regulatory landscapes.
Evaluate India's role in shaping global digital governance norms and promoting open-source digital solutions.
Examine how DPI can accelerate financial inclusion and efficient public service delivery in the Global South.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing interconnected digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and other 'India Stack' components, with global connections.
articles.trivia: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) has revolutionized digital payments in India, processing billions of transactions monthly.

articles.headline: National Animal Disease Control Programme Expands to Cover More Livestock Species
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying announced an expansion of the National Animal Disease Control Programme (NADCP).
• The program, initially focused on Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) and Brucellosis in cattle, will now cover additional livestock species and diseases.
• This expansion aims to enhance animal health, improve livestock productivity, and boost farmers' income.
• Key interventions include mass vaccination, surveillance, disease reporting, and veterinary extension services.
• The initiative is crucial for safeguarding India's vast livestock population and ensuring food security and rural livelihoods.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management
Animal Rearing
articles.source: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying
prelimsPoints: National Animal Disease Control Programme (NADCP) expanded.
Now covers more livestock species and diseases beyond FMD and Brucellosis.
Aims to improve animal health, productivity, and farmers' income.
Key interventions: mass vaccination, surveillance, reporting.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic significance of livestock to India's rural economy and food security.
Analyze the impact of animal diseases on livestock productivity and farmers' livelihoods.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the National Animal Disease Control Programme in mitigating these challenges.
Examine the need for an integrated approach to animal health, including nutrition, breeding, and disease prevention.
articles.imageDescription: Healthy cattle grazing in a green field, symbolizing improved livestock health and productivity.
articles.trivia: India has the largest livestock population in the world, contributing significantly to the agricultural GDP.

articles.headline: Permanent Indus Commission Holds Annual Meeting Amidst Ongoing Water Management Discussions
articles.explainer:
• The Permanent Indus Commission, comprising water commissioners from India and Pakistan, held its annual meeting.
• Discussions focused on the exchange of hydrological data, inspection of projects, and resolution of minor technical issues under the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• The meeting comes at a time of renewed emphasis on cooperative water management and sharing of resources between the two nations.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, a landmark agreement on transboundary water sharing.
• While no major breakthroughs were reported, the regular dialogue is seen as crucial for maintaining stability in water relations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) holds annual meeting.
Body for implementing Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) between India and Pakistan.
IWT governs sharing of Indus River system waters.
Focus on hydrological data exchange and project inspections.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and significance of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the role of the Permanent Indus Commission in ensuring the smooth functioning and dispute resolution under the IWT.
Evaluate the challenges faced in transboundary water management between India and Pakistan, particularly in the context of climate change.
Examine the potential for cooperative water resource management to foster regional stability and economic development.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized map showing the Indus River system flowing through India and Pakistan, with the flags of both countries.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty was brokered by the World Bank and signed in 1960.

articles.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Shows Positive Impact on Early-Stage Startups
articles.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) released an assessment report on the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS).
• The report highlights a positive impact on early-stage startups, enabling them to secure initial funding and validate their ideas.
• SISFS has played a crucial role in fostering innovation, particularly in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, and supporting diverse sectors.
• The scheme provides financial assistance for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• Recommendations include increasing fund allocation and expanding mentorship networks to further strengthen the startup ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life
prelimsPoints: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) assessment report released by DPIIT.
Shows positive impact on early-stage startups.
Aids in proof of concept, prototype development, market entry.
Focus on fostering innovation in Tier 2/3 cities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of government-backed seed funding schemes in nurturing India's startup ecosystem and promoting innovation.
Analyze the challenges faced by early-stage startups in India and how schemes like SISFS address them.
Evaluate the potential of startups to drive economic growth, job creation, and technological advancement in India.
Suggest measures to further strengthen the startup ecosystem, including mentorship, regulatory support, and access to markets.
articles.imageDescription: A group of young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern office space, symbolizing innovation and startup culture.
articles.trivia: India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Launches Initiative for Digital Documentation of Heritage Sites
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) initiated a major project for the digital documentation and preservation of India's archaeological heritage.
• The initiative involves 3D scanning, drone mapping, and virtual reality (VR) technologies to create detailed digital replicas of monuments.
• This will aid in conservation efforts, facilitate research, and enhance public access to culturally significant sites.
• The project also aims to create a comprehensive national digital archive of archaeological findings and historical data.
• Collaboration with technology firms and academic institutions is a key aspect of this ambitious digitalization drive.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Conservation of historical sites
prelimsPoints: ASI launches digital documentation initiative for heritage sites.
Uses 3D scanning, drone mapping, VR technologies.
Aims to aid conservation, research, and public access.
Creating a national digital archive of archaeological findings.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of digital documentation and preservation technologies in safeguarding India's vast cultural and archaeological heritage.
Analyze the benefits of using 3D scanning and virtual reality for heritage conservation, research, and tourism.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in managing and preserving historical sites across the country.
Examine how public-private partnerships and technological advancements can enhance heritage management efforts.
articles.imageDescription: A drone flying over an ancient Indian monument, capturing images for 3D mapping and digital preservation.
articles.trivia: ASI is an attached office of the Ministry of Culture, founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham.

articles.headline: PM Janjati Aadarsh Gram Yojana Shows Progress in Tribal Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs released a progress report on the PM Janjati Aadarsh Gram Yojana (PMJAGY).
• The scheme aims to achieve saturation of various government schemes in tribal-majority villages, focusing on infrastructure and social development.
• Key achievements include improved access to education, healthcare, clean drinking water, and road connectivity in identified villages.
• PMJAGY adopts a holistic approach to tribal development, addressing socio-economic disparities and promoting cultural preservation.
• The report recommends further strengthening convergence with other ministries and enhancing community participation for sustainable impact.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: PM Janjati Aadarsh Gram Yojana (PMJAGY) progress report released.
Aims for saturation of schemes in tribal-majority villages.
Focus on education, healthcare, water, road connectivity.
Implemented by Ministry of Tribal Affairs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic challenges faced by tribal communities in India and the government's efforts to address them.
Analyze the objectives and implementation strategy of the PM Janjati Aadarsh Gram Yojana in promoting inclusive development.
Evaluate the effectiveness of welfare schemes in bringing about sustainable change in tribal areas, considering cultural sensitivities.
Suggest measures for strengthening convergence and community participation to maximize the impact of tribal development programs.
articles.imageDescription: A tribal village scene with children attending school and local infrastructure development in progress.
articles.trivia: The Constitution of India provides special provisions for the administration and control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes.

articles.headline: CERT-In Issues New Guidelines for Enhanced Cyber Security in Critical Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) released updated guidelines for cybersecurity in critical information infrastructure sectors.
• The guidelines mandate stricter compliance for organizations managing essential services like power grids, financial systems, and telecommunications.
• Focus areas include regular security audits, incident response protocols, supply chain security, and employee training.
• The move is aimed at bolstering India's defenses against sophisticated cyber threats and protecting national assets.
• Non-compliance could lead to penalties, emphasizing the government's serious approach to cybersecurity.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Money-laundering and its prevention
prelimsPoints: CERT-In issues new cybersecurity guidelines for Critical Infrastructure.
Mandates stricter compliance for power, finance, telecom sectors.
Focus on security audits, incident response, supply chain security.
Aims to bolster India's defenses against cyber threats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of cyber attacks on critical infrastructure and its implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key provisions of the new CERT-In guidelines and their potential impact on cybersecurity posture of essential services.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing robust cybersecurity frameworks across diverse sectors in India, considering technological gaps and human factors.
Suggest a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, and academia to build a resilient cyber defense ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: A padlock icon overlaid with a circuit board design, symbolizing digital security and critical infrastructure protection.
articles.trivia: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for handling computer security incidents.

articles.headline: G20 Sherpa Meeting Reviews Progress on Sustainable Development Goals Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The latest G20 Sherpa meeting was held, focusing on reviewing the progress made towards achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
• Discussions highlighted the need for accelerated action in areas such as poverty eradication, climate finance, and inclusive growth.
• India, as a prominent voice for the Global South, emphasized equitable access to technology and sustainable development solutions.
• The meeting aimed to build consensus on key policy recommendations to be presented at the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit.
• Sherpas also addressed challenges in global health preparedness and strengthening multilateral development banks.

Focus on poverty eradication, climate finance, inclusive growth.
India advocates for equitable technology access for Global South.

Analyze India's position and contributions within the G20, particularly as a voice for the Global South.
Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance and achieving inclusive growth in the context of current global economic conditions.
Examine the significance of strengthening multilateral development banks in supporting sustainable development efforts worldwide.
articles.imageDescription: The G20 logo with flags of member countries in the background, symbolizing global cooperation on development issues.
articles.trivia: The G20 accounts for about 85% of global GDP, over 75% of global trade, and about two-thirds of the world population.

highlights: Government releases detailed implementation roadmap for National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming for global leadership.
Sanjay Roy convicted in 2024 Kolkata rape and murder case, emphasizing swift justice in heinous crimes.
RBI maintains repo rate amidst global economic uncertainties, balancing growth and inflation control.
India and Japan hold strategic dialogue, focusing on economic security and Indo-Pacific stability.
ISRO accelerates preparations for Chandrayaan-4, India's ambitious lunar sample return mission.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It is implemented by the Ministry of Power.
2. Its primary objective is to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
3. It focuses on developing demand-side applications in sectors like fertilizers and steel.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The National Green Hydrogen Mission is implemented by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE). Statements 2 and 3 are correct as the mission aims to make India a global hub and focuses on demand-side applications in hard-to-abate sectors.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for implementing the National Animal Disease Control Programme (NADCP)?
options: (a) Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
(b) Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
(c) Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Animal Disease Control Programme (NADCP) is implemented by the Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying. It aims to control and eradicate diseases like Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) and Brucellosis in livestock.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT):
1. It was signed between India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh.
2. The Permanent Indus Commission is responsible for its implementation.
3. The treaty primarily allocates the waters of the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960. Statement 2 is correct, the Permanent Indus Commission is tasked with its implementation. Statement 3 is also correct; the treaty allocates the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India for unrestricted use, while the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) are largely for Pakistan.

prelimsMocks.question: INS Vikrant, recently in news, is significant because it is:
options: (d) India's first indigenously-developed ballistic missile defense system.
prelimsMocks.explanation: INS Vikrant is India's first indigenously-built aircraft carrier, marking a significant milestone in India's self-reliance in defense manufacturing and enhancing its naval power projection capabilities.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following 'India Stack' components are being actively promoted by India for global partnerships in Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)?
1. Aadhaar
2. Goods and Services Tax Network (GSTN)
3. Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
4. DigiLocker

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar (identity), UPI (payments), and DigiLocker (data exchange) are core components of the 'India Stack' being actively promoted for global partnerships in Digital Public Infrastructure. While GSTN is a crucial part of India's digital infrastructure, it is not typically cited as a general-purpose DPI component for export in the same way as identity, payments, or data exchange platforms.

mainsMocks.question: The National Green Hydrogen Mission is a cornerstone for India's energy transition and climate commitments. Discuss its strategic importance and analyze the challenges involved in achieving its ambitious targets.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental Pollution and Degradation; Conservation; Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its objectives. Elaborate on its strategic importance for energy security, decarbonization, economic growth, and global leadership. Then, detail the challenges such as high production costs, technology scaling, infrastructure development, water availability, and policy implementation. Conclude with measures for overcoming these challenges.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's engagement with the Electoral Bonds Scheme highlights the perennial debate on transparency in political funding. Critically evaluate the arguments for and against the scheme, and suggest comprehensive reforms needed to ensure free and fair elections in India.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions; Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the context of the Supreme Court's hearing on the Electoral Bonds Scheme. Present arguments in favor (donor privacy, curbing black money) and against (lack of transparency, potential for quid pro quo, impact on democracy). Discuss the implications for free and fair elections. Conclude by suggesting electoral reforms such as a National Electoral Fund, state funding, and stricter disclosure norms.

mainsMocks.question: India's growing capabilities in space exploration, exemplified by missions like Chandrayaan-4, have significant implications for its scientific prowess and geopolitical standing. Discuss the objectives and technological complexities of lunar sample return missions and evaluate India's trajectory in space exploration.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life; Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Awareness in the fields of Space.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Chandrayaan-4 as India's planned lunar sample return mission. Discuss the scientific objectives (understanding lunar geology, resource potential) and the technological complexities involved (precision landing, robotic sample collection, return vehicle design, re-entry). Evaluate ISRO's track record and future trajectory in space exploration, highlighting its 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' approach and contributions to global space science.

quickBytes.text: The National Green Hydrogen Mission is overseen by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
Sanjay Roy was convicted in the 2024 Kolkata rape and murder case by the Sealdah court.
The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the benchmark repo rate.
India and Japan are key partners in the Quad grouping, focusing on Indo-Pacific security.
Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as India's first lunar sample return mission by ISRO.
The National Curriculum Framework (NCF) aligns with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
The Gangetic Dolphin is India's National Aquatic Animal and is protected under wildlife laws.
INS Vikrant is India's first indigenously designed and built aircraft carrier.
The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was mediated by the World Bank between India and Pakistan.
CERT-In is the national agency for handling computer security incidents in India.

GS1.headline: Sanjay Roy Convicted in 2024 Kolkata Rape and Murder Case by Sealdah Court
GS1.explainer:
• Sanjay Roy has been convicted by the Sealdah court in Kolkata, West Bengal, for the 2024 Kolkata rape and murder case.
• The verdict comes after a fast-tracked trial, highlighting the judiciary's commitment to swift justice in heinous crimes.
• The case had drawn significant public attention and outrage, prompting calls for stricter laws and better law enforcement.
• Legal experts note the importance of forensic evidence and witness testimonies in securing the conviction.
• The judgment is expected to be followed by sentencing, which will determine the quantum of punishment.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization
Social empowerment
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in ensuring swift justice in cases of sexual assault and murder in India.
Analyze the role of public outcry and media in expediting judicial processes in high-profile cases.
Examine the effectiveness of existing laws and judicial mechanisms in protecting women and delivering justice.
Suggest reforms needed in the criminal justice system to prevent such crimes and ensure timely convictions.
GS1.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a law book with a blurred background of a court building, symbolizing justice.
GS1.trivia: India's first all-women police station was established in Kozhikode, Kerala, in 1973.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Society
prelimsSyllabus.area: Role of women and women’s organization

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Launches Initiative for Digital Documentation of Heritage Sites
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) initiated a major project for the digital documentation and preservation of India's archaeological heritage.
• The initiative involves 3D scanning, drone mapping, and virtual reality (VR) technologies to create detailed digital replicas of monuments.
• This will aid in conservation efforts, facilitate research, and enhance public access to culturally significant sites.
• The project also aims to create a comprehensive national digital archive of archaeological findings and historical data.
• Collaboration with technology firms and academic institutions is a key aspect of this ambitious digitalization drive.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Conservation of historical sites
prelimsPoints: ASI launches digital documentation initiative for heritage sites.
Uses 3D scanning, drone mapping, VR technologies.
Aims to aid conservation, research, and public access.
Creating a national digital archive of archaeological findings.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of digital documentation and preservation technologies in safeguarding India's vast cultural and archaeological heritage.
Analyze the benefits of using 3D scanning and virtual reality for heritage conservation, research, and tourism.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in managing and preserving historical sites across the country.
Examine how public-private partnerships and technological advancements can enhance heritage management efforts.
GS1.imageDescription: A drone flying over an ancient Indian monument, capturing images for 3D mapping and digital preservation.
GS1.trivia: ASI is an attached office of the Ministry of Culture, founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Hold Strategic Dialogue, Emphasize Economic Security and Indo-Pacific Stability
GS2.explainer:
• High-level delegations from India and Japan held their annual Strategic Dialogue in New Delhi, focusing on enhancing bilateral cooperation.
• Discussions centered on strengthening economic security, supply chain resilience, and collaborative efforts for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to deepening defense cooperation, including joint exercises and technology transfers.
• Key areas of collaboration identified include critical and emerging technologies, green energy, and infrastructure development.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of interests between India and Japan in regional and global affairs.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue held in New Delhi.
Focus areas include economic security, Indo-Pacific stability, defense cooperation.
Collaboration in critical technologies, green energy, infrastructure.
Both nations are key partners in the Quad grouping.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-Japan strategic partnership in ensuring stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how enhanced economic security cooperation between India and Japan can mitigate global supply chain vulnerabilities.
Evaluate the potential for India and Japan to collaborate on critical and emerging technologies (CETs) and its implications for global technological leadership.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in deepening defense ties between the two countries.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed prominently, symbolizing diplomatic relations.
GS2.trivia: Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects, notably the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail corridor.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Hear Petitions Seeking Greater Transparency in Electoral Bonds Scheme
GS2.explainer:
• A special bench of the Supreme Court is scheduled to hear a batch of petitions challenging the Electoral Bonds Scheme.
• The petitions argue that the scheme lacks transparency, potentially leading to anonymous funding of political parties and impacting free and fair elections.
• Petitioners advocate for disclosure of donor identities to ensure accountability and prevent quid pro quo arrangements.
• The government has previously defended the scheme, citing donor privacy and curbing black money in political funding.
• The outcome of these hearings could have significant implications for political funding reforms and electoral transparency in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court hearing petitions on Electoral Bonds Scheme.
Petitions challenge the scheme on grounds of lack of transparency.
Government defends the scheme citing donor privacy.
Electoral bonds introduced in 2018.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds Scheme in the context of electoral transparency and democratic principles.
Discuss the role of the judiciary in upholding the principles of free and fair elections in India.
Analyze the impact of anonymous political funding on electoral integrity and the potential for corruption.
Suggest alternative models for political funding that balance transparency with donor privacy and curb the use of black money.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building with legal documents and a balance scale in the foreground, symbolizing justice and law.
GS2.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from 1951 to 1952.

GS2.headline: India-Central Asia Summit Focuses on Enhanced Connectivity and Regional Security
GS2.explainer:
• Leaders from India and five Central Asian nations convened for a virtual summit, emphasizing closer cooperation in various sectors.
• A major theme was the enhancement of connectivity, including proposals for new transport corridors and digital linkages.
• Discussions also covered regional security challenges, counter-terrorism efforts, and the situation in Afghanistan.
• Cultural and people-to-people exchanges were highlighted as crucial for strengthening bilateral ties.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration affirming commitment to a comprehensive and sustained partnership.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Central Asia Summit held.
Focus on connectivity, regional security, counter-terrorism.
Central Asian nations: Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan.
Joint declaration issued for comprehensive partnership.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Central Asia for India's foreign policy and economic interests.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in developing robust connectivity pathways between India and Central Asian countries.
Examine the role of India-Central Asia cooperation in addressing regional security concerns, particularly regarding Afghanistan.
Evaluate India's 'Connect Central Asia' policy and its achievements so far.
GS2.imageDescription: A world map highlighting India and the Central Asian region, with lines indicating trade routes and connectivity.
GS2.trivia: The ancient Silk Road historically connected India with Central Asia, facilitating trade and cultural exchange.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests

GS2.headline: National Curriculum Framework Implementation Begins with Focus on Foundational Learning
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced the phased implementation of the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for school education.
• The initial phase focuses on foundational stage education, emphasizing activity-based learning, multilingualism, and holistic development.
• NCF aligns with the recommendations of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, aiming for a flexible, multidisciplinary, and experiential learning approach.
• Teacher training and development of new pedagogical resources are critical components of this implementation.
• The framework seeks to reduce rote learning and foster critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving skills among students.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: National Curriculum Framework (NCF) implementation begins.
Focus on foundational stage education.
Aligned with National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Emphasizes activity-based learning and multilingualism.
Aims to reduce rote learning.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key features and objectives of the National Curriculum Framework (NCF) in line with NEP 2020.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a new curriculum framework across diverse educational ecosystems in India.
Evaluate the potential impact of NCF on student learning outcomes, critical thinking, and holistic development.
Examine the role of teacher training and technological integration in the successful adoption of the new curriculum.
GS2.imageDescription: Children in a classroom engaged in interactive learning, with colorful educational materials.
GS2.trivia: The first National Education Policy in India was formulated in 1968.

GS2.headline: India Forges Global Partnerships to Export Digital Public Infrastructure Model
GS2.explainer:
• India is actively pursuing bilateral and multilateral partnerships to share its successful Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model with other developing nations.
• The 'India Stack' components like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are being showcased as examples of scalable and inclusive digital solutions.
• The initiative aims to foster digital transformation in partner countries, promoting financial inclusion and efficient public service delivery.
• This diplomatic push positions India as a leader in digital governance and a proponent of open-source, interoperable digital frameworks.
• Capacity building and technical assistance are key aspects of these partnerships, ensuring effective adoption and localization.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
GS2.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
prelimsPoints: India exporting its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model.
Key components: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker (India Stack).
Aims to promote digital transformation and financial inclusion in partner countries.
Positions India as a leader in digital governance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model as a global public good for developing nations.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of exporting the 'India Stack' to countries with diverse technological and regulatory landscapes.
Evaluate India's role in shaping global digital governance norms and promoting open-source digital solutions.
Examine how DPI can accelerate financial inclusion and efficient public service delivery in the Global South.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing interconnected digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and other 'India Stack' components, with global connections.
GS2.trivia: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) has revolutionized digital payments in India, processing billions of transactions monthly.

GS2.headline: Permanent Indus Commission Holds Annual Meeting Amidst Ongoing Water Management Discussions
GS2.explainer:
• The Permanent Indus Commission, comprising water commissioners from India and Pakistan, held its annual meeting.
• Discussions focused on the exchange of hydrological data, inspection of projects, and resolution of minor technical issues under the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• The meeting comes at a time of renewed emphasis on cooperative water management and sharing of resources between the two nations.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, a landmark agreement on transboundary water sharing.
• While no major breakthroughs were reported, the regular dialogue is seen as crucial for maintaining stability in water relations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) holds annual meeting.
Body for implementing Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) between India and Pakistan.
IWT governs sharing of Indus River system waters.
Focus on hydrological data exchange and project inspections.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and significance of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the role of the Permanent Indus Commission in ensuring the smooth functioning and dispute resolution under the IWT.
Evaluate the challenges faced in transboundary water management between India and Pakistan, particularly in the context of climate change.
Examine the potential for cooperative water resource management to foster regional stability and economic development.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized map showing the Indus River system flowing through India and Pakistan, with the flags of both countries.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty was brokered by the World Bank and signed in 1960.

GS2.headline: PM Janjati Aadarsh Gram Yojana Shows Progress in Tribal Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs released a progress report on the PM Janjati Aadarsh Gram Yojana (PMJAGY).
• The scheme aims to achieve saturation of various government schemes in tribal-majority villages, focusing on infrastructure and social development.
• Key achievements include improved access to education, healthcare, clean drinking water, and road connectivity in identified villages.
• PMJAGY adopts a holistic approach to tribal development, addressing socio-economic disparities and promoting cultural preservation.
• The report recommends further strengthening convergence with other ministries and enhancing community participation for sustainable impact.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: PM Janjati Aadarsh Gram Yojana (PMJAGY) progress report released.
Aims for saturation of schemes in tribal-majority villages.
Focus on education, healthcare, water, road connectivity.
Implemented by Ministry of Tribal Affairs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic challenges faced by tribal communities in India and the government's efforts to address them.
Analyze the objectives and implementation strategy of the PM Janjati Aadarsh Gram Yojana in promoting inclusive development.
Evaluate the effectiveness of welfare schemes in bringing about sustainable change in tribal areas, considering cultural sensitivities.
Suggest measures for strengthening convergence and community participation to maximize the impact of tribal development programs.
GS2.imageDescription: A tribal village scene with children attending school and local infrastructure development in progress.
GS2.trivia: The Constitution of India provides special provisions for the administration and control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes.

GS2.headline: G20 Sherpa Meeting Reviews Progress on Sustainable Development Goals Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The latest G20 Sherpa meeting was held, focusing on reviewing the progress made towards achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
• Discussions highlighted the need for accelerated action in areas such as poverty eradication, climate finance, and inclusive growth.
• India, as a prominent voice for the Global South, emphasized equitable access to technology and sustainable development solutions.
• The meeting aimed to build consensus on key policy recommendations to be presented at the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit.
• Sherpas also addressed challenges in global health preparedness and strengthening multilateral development banks.

Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance and achieving inclusive growth in the context of current global economic conditions.
Examine the significance of strengthening multilateral development banks in supporting sustainable development efforts worldwide.
